verses.latin_vulgate: Cernens autem Rachel quod infecunda esset, invidit sorori suæ, et ait marito suo : Da mihi liberos, alioquin moriar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֣רֶא רָחֵ֗ל כִּ֣י לֹ֤א יָֽלְדָה֙ לְיַעֲקֹ֔ב וַתְּקַנֵּ֥א רָחֵ֖ל בַּאֲחֹתָ֑הּ וַתֹּ֤אמֶר אֶֽל־יַעֲקֹב֙ הָֽבָה־לִּ֣י בָנִ֔ים וְאִם־אַ֖יִן מֵתָ֥ה אָנֹֽכִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Rachel seeing herself without children, envied her sister, and said to her husband: Give me children, otherwise I shall die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Rakel näki, ettei hän synnyttänyt Jakobille, kadehti hän sisartansa, ja sanoi Jakobille: anna minun lapsia, eli minä kuolen.
verses.king_james: And when Rachel saw that she bare Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister; and said unto Jacob, Give me children, or else I die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui iratus respondit Jacob : Num pro Deo ego sum, qui privavit te fructu ventris tui ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽחַר־אַ֥ף יַעֲקֹ֖ב בְּרָחֵ֑ל וַיֹּ֗אמֶר הֲתַ֤חַת אֱלֹהִים֙ אָנֹ֔כִי אֲשֶׁר־מָנַ֥ע מִמֵּ֖ךְ פְּרִי־בָֽטֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob being angry with her, answered: Am I as God, who hath deprived thee of the fruit of thy womb?
verses.finnish_biblia: Jakob vihastui kovin Rakelin päälle, ja sanoi: olenko minä Jumalan siassa, joka on sinulta kieltänyt kohdun hedelmän?
verses.king_james: And Jacob’s anger was kindled against Rachel: and he said, Am I in God’s stead, who hath withheld from thee the fruit of the womb?

verses.latin_vulgate: At illa : Habeo, inquit, famulam Balam : ingredere ad illam, ut pariat super genua mea, et habeam ex illa filios.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֕אמֶר הִנֵּ֛ה אֲמָתִ֥י בִלְהָ֖ה בֹּ֣א אֵלֶ֑יהָ וְתֵלֵד֙ עַל־בִּרְכַּ֔י וְאִבָּנֶ֥ה גַם־אָנֹכִ֖י מִמֶּֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But she said: I have here my servant Bala: go in unto her, that she may bear upon my knees, and I may have children by her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän sanoi: tässä on minun piikani Bilha, makaa hänen kanssansa; että hän synnyttäis minun helmaani, ja saisin sittenkin hänestä lapsia.
verses.king_james: And she said, Behold my maid Bilhah, go in unto her; and she shall bear upon my knees, that I may also have children by her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deditque illi Balam in conjugium : quæ,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּתֶּן־ל֛וֹ אֶת־בִּלְהָ֥ה שִׁפְחָתָ֖הּ לְאִשָּׁ֑ה וַיָּבֹ֥א אֵלֶ֖יהָ יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she gave him Bala in marriage: who,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän antoi hänelle piikansa Bilhan emännäksi: ja Jakob makasi hänen kanssansa.
verses.king_james: And she gave him Bilhah her handmaid to wife: and Jacob went in unto her.

verses.latin_vulgate: ingresso ad se viro, concepit, et peperit filium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֣הַר בִּלְהָ֔ה וַתֵּ֥לֶד לְיַעֲקֹ֖ב בֵּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: When her husband had gone in unto her, conceived and bore a son.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Bilha tuli raskaaksi ja synnytti Jakobille pojan.
verses.king_james: And Bilhah conceived, and bare Jacob a son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Rachel : Judicavit mihi Dominus, et exaudivit vocem meam, dans mihi filium, et idcirco appellavit nomen ejus Dan.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֤אמֶר רָחֵל֙ דָּנַ֣נִּי אֱלֹהִ֔ים וְגַם֙ שָׁמַ֣ע בְּקֹלִ֔י וַיִּתֶּן־לִ֖י בֵּ֑ן עַל־כֵּ֛ן קָרְאָ֥ה שְׁמ֖וֹ דָּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Rachel said: The Lord hath judged for me, and hath heard my voice, giving me a son; and therefore she called his name Dan.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Rakel sanoi: Jumala ratkasi minun asiani, ja kuuli myös minun ääneni, ja antoi minulle pojan; sentähden kutsui hän hänen nimensä Dan.
verses.king_james: And Rachel said, God hath judged me, and hath also heard my voice, and hath given me a son: therefore called she his name Dan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rursumque Bala concipiens, peperit alterum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֣הַר ע֔וֹד וַתֵּ֕לֶד בִּלְהָ֖ה שִׁפְחַ֣ת רָחֵ֑ל בֵּ֥ן שֵׁנִ֖י לְיַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And again Bala conceived, and bore another,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja taas Bilha Rakelin piika tuli raskaaksi, ja synnytti Jakobille toisen pojan.
verses.king_james: And Bilhah Rachel’s maid conceived again, and bare Jacob a second son.

verses.latin_vulgate: pro quo ait Rachel : Comparavit me Deus cum sorore mea, et invalui : vocavitque eum Nephthali.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר רָחֵ֗ל נַפְתּוּלֵ֨י אֱלֹהִ֧ים ׀ נִפְתַּ֛לְתִּי עִם־אֲחֹתִ֖י גַּם־יָכֹ֑לְתִּי וַתִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖וֹ נַפְתָּלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For whom Rachel said: God hath compared me with my sister, and I have prevailed: and she called him Nephthali.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Rakel: minä olen jalosti kilvoitellut minun sisareni kanssa, ja olen myös voittanut: ja hän kutsui hänen nimensä Naphtali.
verses.king_james: And Rachel said, With great wrestlings have I wrestled with my sister, and I have prevailed: and she called his name Naphtali.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sentiens Lia quod parere desiisset, Zelpham ancillam suam marito tradidit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֣רֶא לֵאָ֔ה כִּ֥י עָמְדָ֖ה מִלֶּ֑דֶת וַתִּקַּח֙ אֶת־זִלְפָּ֣ה שִׁפְחָתָ֔הּ וַתִּתֵּ֥ן אֹתָ֛הּ לְיַעֲקֹ֖ב לְאִשָּֽׁה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Lia perceiving that she had left of bearing, gave Zelpha, her handmaid, to her husband.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Lea näki, että hän lakkasi synnyttämästä; otti hän piikansa Silpan, ja antoi sen Jakobille emännäksi.
verses.king_james: When Leah saw that she had left bearing, she took Zilpah her maid, and gave her Jacob to wife.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qua post conceptum edente filium,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֗לֶד זִלְפָּ֛ה שִׁפְחַ֥ת לֵאָ֖ה לְיַעֲקֹ֥ב בֵּֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when she had conceived, and brought forth a son,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin synnytti Silpa, Lean piika, Jakobille pojan.
verses.king_james: And Zilpah Leah’s maid bare Jacob a son.

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit : Feliciter, et idcirco vocavit nomen ejus Gad.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֥אמֶר לֵאָ֖ה (בגד) [בָּ֣א גָ֑ד] וַתִּקְרָ֥א אֶת־שְׁמ֖וֹ גָּֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: She said: Happily. And therefore called his name Gad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Lea sanoi: joukko tulee: ja kutsui hänen nimensä Gad.
verses.king_james: And Leah said, A troop cometh: and she called his name Gad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Peperit quoque Zelpha alterum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֵּ֗לֶד זִלְפָּה֙ שִׁפְחַ֣ת לֵאָ֔ה בֵּ֥ן שֵׁנִ֖י לְיַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: Zelpha also bore another.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Silpa, Lean piika, synnytti Jakobille toisen pojan.
verses.king_james: And Zilpah Leah’s maid bare Jacob a second son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Lia : Hoc pro beatitudine mea : beatam quippe me dicent mulieres : propterea appellavit eum Aser.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר לֵאָ֔ה בְּאׇשְׁרִ֕י כִּ֥י אִשְּׁר֖וּנִי בָּנ֑וֹת וַתִּקְרָ֥א אֶת־שְׁמ֖וֹ אָשֵֽׁר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Lia said: This is for my happiness: for women will call me blessed. Therefore she called him Aser.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Lea sanoi: autuasta minua, sillä tyttäret sanovat minua autuaaksi: ja hän kutsui hänen nimensä Asser.
verses.king_james: And Leah said, Happy am I, for the daughters will call me blessed: and she called his name Asher.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressus autem Ruben tempore messis triticeæ in agrum, reperit mandragoras, quas matri Liæ detulit. Dixitque Rachel : Da mihi partem de mandragoris filii tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֨לֶךְ רְאוּבֵ֜ן בִּימֵ֣י קְצִיר־חִטִּ֗ים וַיִּמְצָ֤א דֽוּדָאִים֙ בַּשָּׂדֶ֔ה וַיָּבֵ֣א אֹתָ֔ם אֶל־לֵאָ֖ה אִמּ֑וֹ וַתֹּ֤אמֶר רָחֵל֙ אֶל־לֵאָ֔ה תְּנִי־נָ֣א לִ֔י מִדּוּדָאֵ֖י בְּנֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ruben going out in the time of the wheat harvest into the field, found mandrakes: which he brought to his mother Lia. And Rachel said: Give me part of thy son's mandrakes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ruben meni ulos nisun elonaikana, ja löysi dudaimia vainioilta, ja toi ne Lealle äidillensä. Niin sanoi Rakel Lealle: anna minulle poikas dudaimista.
verses.king_james: And Reuben went in the days of wheat harvest, and found mandrakes in the field, and brought them unto his mother Leah. Then Rachel said to Leah, Give me, I pray thee, of thy son’s mandrakes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illa respondit : Parumne tibi videtur quod præripueris maritum mihi, nisi etiam mandragoras filii mei tuleris ? Ait Rachel : Dormiat tecum hac nocte pro mandragoris filii tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר לָ֗הּ הַמְעַט֙ קַחְתֵּ֣ךְ אֶת־אִישִׁ֔י וְלָקַ֕חַת גַּ֥ם אֶת־דּוּדָאֵ֖י בְּנִ֑י וַתֹּ֣אמֶר רָחֵ֗ל לָכֵן֙ יִשְׁכַּ֤ב עִמָּךְ֙ הַלַּ֔יְלָה תַּ֖חַת דּוּדָאֵ֥י בְנֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: She answered: Dost thou think it a small matter, that thou hast taken my husband from me, unless thou take also my son's mandrakes? Rachel said: He shall sleep with thee this night, for thy son's mandrakes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän vastasi häntä: vähäkö sinun siinä on, ettäs olet minulta miehen vienyt, mutta tahdot myös ottaa poikani dudaimit? Ja Rakel sanoi: sentähden maatkaan hän tänä yönä sinun kanssas poikas dudaimien tähden.
verses.king_james: And she said unto her, Is it a small matter that thou hast taken my husband? and wouldest thou take away my son’s mandrakes also? And Rachel said, Therefore he shall lie with thee to night for thy son’s mandrakes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Redeuntique ad vesperam Jacob de agro, egressa est in occursum ejus Lia, et Ad me, inquit, intrabis : quia mercede conduxi te pro mandragoris filii mei. Dormivitque cum ea nocte illa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֨א יַעֲקֹ֣ב מִן־הַשָּׂדֶה֮ בָּעֶ֒רֶב֒ וַתֵּצֵ֨א לֵאָ֜ה לִקְרָאת֗וֹ וַתֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אֵלַ֣י תָּב֔וֹא כִּ֚י שָׂכֹ֣ר שְׂכַרְתִּ֔יךָ בְּדוּדָאֵ֖י בְּנִ֑י וַיִּשְׁכַּ֥ב עִמָּ֖הּ בַּלַּ֥יְלָה הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Jacob returned at even from the field, Lia went out to meet him, and said: Thou shalt come in unto me, because I have hired thee for my son's mandrakes. And he slept with her that night.
verses.finnish_biblia: Koska Jakob ehtoona kedolta palasi, meni Lea häntä vastaan, ja sanoi: minun kanssani pitää sinun makaaman: sillä minä olen sinun kallisti ostanut poikani dudaimilla. Ja hän makasi sen yön hänen kanssansa.
verses.king_james: And Jacob came out of the field in the evening, and Leah went out to meet him, and said, Thou must come in unto me; for surely I have hired thee with my son’s mandrakes. And he lay with her that night.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exaudivit Deus preces ejus, concepitque et peperit filium quintum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֥ע אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶל־לֵאָ֑ה וַתַּ֛הַר וַתֵּ֥לֶד לְיַעֲקֹ֖ב בֵּ֥ן חֲמִישִֽׁי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And God heard her prayers; and she conceived: and bore a fifth son:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jumala kuuli Lean, ja hän tuli raskaaksi, ja synnytti Jakobille viidennen pojan.
verses.king_james: And God hearkened unto Leah, and she conceived, and bare Jacob the fifth son.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait : Dedit Deus mercedem mihi, quia dedi ancillam meam viro meo : appellavitque nomen ejus Issachar.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר לֵאָ֗ה נָתַ֤ן אֱלֹהִים֙ שְׂכָרִ֔י אֲשֶׁר־נָתַ֥תִּי שִׁפְחָתִ֖י לְאִישִׁ֑י וַתִּקְרָ֥א שְׁמ֖וֹ יִשָּׂשכָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And said: God hath given me a reward, because I gave my handmaid to my husband. And she called his name Issachar.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Lea sanoi: Jumala on maksanut sen minulle, että minä annoin piikani miehelleni: ja kutsui hänen nimensä Isaskar.
verses.king_james: And Leah said, God hath given me my hire, because I have given my maiden to my husband: and she called his name Issachar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rursum Lia concipiens, peperit sextum filium,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֤הַר עוֹד֙ לֵאָ֔ה וַתֵּ֥לֶד בֵּן־שִׁשִּׁ֖י לְיַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Lia conceived again, and bore the sixth son,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Lea taas tuli raskaaksi, ja synnytti Jakobille kuudennen pojan.
verses.king_james: And Leah conceived again, and bare Jacob the sixth son.

verses.latin_vulgate: et ait : Dotavit me Deus dote bona : etiam hac vice mecum erit maritus meus, eo quod genuerim ei sex filios : et idcirco appellavit nomen ejus Zabulon.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּ֣אמֶר לֵאָ֗ה זְבָדַ֨נִי אֱלֹהִ֥ים ׀ אֹתִי֮ זֵ֣בֶד טוֹב֒ הַפַּ֙עַם֙ יִזְבְּלֵ֣נִי אִישִׁ֔י כִּֽי־יָלַ֥דְתִּי ל֖וֹ שִׁשָּׁ֣ה בָנִ֑ים וַתִּקְרָ֥א אֶת־שְׁמ֖וֹ זְבֻלֽוּן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And said: God hath endowed me with a good dowry; this turn also my husband will be with me, because I have borne him six sons: and therefore she called his name Zabulon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Lea sanoi: Jumala on minun hyvästi lahjoittanut, nyt taas asuu minun mieheni minun tykönäni: sillä minä olen synnyttänyt hänelle kuusi poikaa. Ja kutsui hänen nimensä Sebulon.
verses.king_james: And Leah said, God hath endued me with a good dowry; now will my husband dwell with me, because I have born him six sons: and she called his name Zebulun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post quem peperit filiam, nomine Dinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַחַ֖ר יָ֣לְדָה בַּ֑ת וַתִּקְרָ֥א אֶת־שְׁמָ֖הּ דִּינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: After whom she bore a daughter, named Dina.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte synnytti hän tyttären, ja kutsui hänen nimensä Dina.
verses.king_james: And afterwards she bare a daughter, and called her name Dinah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recordatus quoque Dominus Rachelis, exaudivit eam, et aperuit vulvam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּזְכֹּ֥ר אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶת־רָחֵ֑ל וַיִּשְׁמַ֤ע אֵלֶ֙יהָ֙ אֱלֹהִ֔ים וַיִּפְתַּ֖ח אֶת־רַחְמָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Lord also remembering Rachel, heard her, and opened her womb.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Jumala muisti myös Rakelin, ja Jumala kuuli hänen, ja saatti hedelmälliseksi.
verses.king_james: And God remembered Rachel, and God hearkened to her, and opened her womb.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ concepit, et peperit filium, dicens : Abstulit Deus opprobrium meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתַּ֖הַר וַתֵּ֣לֶד בֵּ֑ן וַתֹּ֕אמֶר אָסַ֥ף אֱלֹהִ֖ים אֶת־חֶרְפָּתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she conceived, and bore a son, saying: God hath taken away my reproach.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin hän tuli raskaaksi ja synnytti pojan, ja sanoi: Jumala on ottanut pois minun häväistykseni.
verses.king_james: And she conceived, and bare a son; and said, God hath taken away my reproach:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vocavit nomen ejus Joseph, dicens : Addat mihi Dominus filium alterum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתִּקְרָ֧א אֶת־שְׁמ֛וֹ יוֹסֵ֖ף לֵאמֹ֑ר יֹסֵ֧ף יְהֹוָ֛ה לִ֖י בֵּ֥ן אַחֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she called his name Joseph: saying: The Lord give me also another son.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kutsui hänen nimensä Joseph, sanoen: Herra lisätköön minulle vielä toisen pojan.
verses.king_james: And she called his name Joseph; and said, The Lord shall add to me another son.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nato autem Joseph, dixit Jacob socero suo : Dimitte me ut revertar in patriam, et ad terram meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֕י כַּאֲשֶׁ֛ר יָלְדָ֥ה רָחֵ֖ל אֶת־יוֹסֵ֑ף וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יַעֲקֹב֙ אֶל־לָבָ֔ן שַׁלְּחֵ֙נִי֙ וְאֵ֣לְכָ֔ה אֶל־מְקוֹמִ֖י וּלְאַרְצִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when Joseph was born, Jacob said to his father-in-law: Send me away, that I may return into my country, and to my land.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui, koska Rakel oli synnyttänyt Josephin; sanoi Jakob Labanille: päästä minua menemään kotiani ja maalleni.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, when Rachel had born Joseph, that Jacob said unto Laban, Send me away, that I may go unto mine own place, and to my country.

verses.latin_vulgate: Da mihi uxores, et liberos meos, pro quibus servivi tibi, ut abeam : tu nosti servitutem qua servivi tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּנָ֞ה אֶת־נָשַׁ֣י וְאֶת־יְלָדַ֗י אֲשֶׁ֨ר עָבַ֧דְתִּי אֹֽתְךָ֛ בָּהֵ֖ן וְאֵלֵ֑כָה כִּ֚י אַתָּ֣ה יָדַ֔עְתָּ אֶת־עֲבֹדָתִ֖י אֲשֶׁ֥ר עֲבַדְתִּֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Give me my wives, and my children, for whom I have served thee, that I may depart: thou knowest the service that I have rendered thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Anna minulle minun emäntäni ja lapseni, joiden tähden minä olen sinua palvellut, että minä menisin pois: sillä sinä tiedät minun palvelukseni, kuin minä olen sinua palvellut.
verses.king_james: Give me my wives and my children, for whom I have served thee, and let me go: for thou knowest my service which I have done thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait illi Laban : Inveniam gratiam in conspectu tuo, experimento didici, quia benedixerit mihi Deus propter te :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָיו֙ לָבָ֔ן אִם־נָ֛א מָצָ֥אתִי חֵ֖ן בְּעֵינֶ֑יךָ נִחַ֕שְׁתִּי וַיְבָרְכֵ֥נִי יְהֹוָ֖ה בִּגְלָלֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Laban said to him: Let me find favour in thy sight: I have learned, by experience, that God hath blessed me for thy sake.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Laban sanoi hänelle: anna minun löytää armo sinun edessäs, minä ymmärrän, että Herra on siunannut minun sinun tähtes.
verses.king_james: And Laban said unto him, I pray thee, if I have found favour in thine eyes, tarry: for I have learned by experience that the Lord hath blessed me for thy sake.

verses.latin_vulgate: constitue mercedem tuam quam dem tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמַ֑ר נׇקְבָ֧ה שְׂכָרְךָ֛ עָלַ֖י וְאֶתֵּֽנָה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Appoint thy wages which I shall give thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sanoi (vielä): määrää siis sinun palkkas minulle, ja minä annan.
verses.king_james: And he said, Appoint me thy wages, and I will give it.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondit : Tu nosti quomodo servierim tibi, et quanta in manibus meis fuerit possessio tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֵלָ֔יו אַתָּ֣ה יָדַ֔עְתָּ אֵ֖ת אֲשֶׁ֣ר עֲבַדְתִּ֑יךָ וְאֵ֛ת אֲשֶׁר־הָיָ֥ה מִקְנְךָ֖ אִתִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: But he answered: Thou knowest how I have served thee, and how great thy possession hath been in my hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän sanoi hänelle: sinä tiedät, kuinka minä olen palvellut sinua: ja kuinka paljo sinun karjaas on minun haltuuni annettu.
verses.king_james: And he said unto him, Thou knowest how I have served thee, and how thy cattle was with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Modicum habuisti antequam venirem ad te, et nunc dives effectus es : benedixitque tibi Dominus ad introitum meum. Justum est igitur ut aliquando provideam etiam domui meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֡י מְעַט֩ אֲשֶׁר־הָיָ֨ה לְךָ֤ לְפָנַי֙ וַיִּפְרֹ֣ץ לָרֹ֔ב וַיְבָ֧רֶךְ יְהֹוָ֛ה אֹתְךָ֖ לְרַגְלִ֑י וְעַתָּ֗ה מָתַ֛י אֶֽעֱשֶׂ֥ה גַם־אָנֹכִ֖י לְבֵיתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou hadst but little before I came to thee, and now thou art become rich: and the Lord hath blessed thee at my coming. It is reasonable, therefore, that I should now provide also for my own house.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinulla oli vähä ennenkuin minä tulin, mutta nyt se on paljoksi kasvanut, ja Herra on siunannut sinun, minun jalkajuonteni kautta. Koska minä siis oman huoneeni parasta katson?
verses.king_james: For it was little which thou hadst before I came, and it is now increased unto a multitude; and the Lord hath blessed thee since my coming: and now when shall I provide for mine own house also?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Laban : Quid tibi dabo ? At ille ait : Nihil volo : sed si feceris quod postulo, iterum pascam, et custodiam pecora tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֖אמֶר מָ֣ה אֶתֶּן־לָ֑ךְ וַיֹּ֤אמֶר יַעֲקֹב֙ לֹא־תִתֶּן־לִ֣י מְא֔וּמָה אִם־תַּֽעֲשֶׂה־לִּי֙ הַדָּבָ֣ר הַזֶּ֔ה אָשׁ֛וּבָה אֶרְעֶ֥ה צֹֽאנְךָ֖ אֶשְׁמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Laban said: What shall I give thee? But he said: I require nothing; but if thou wilt do what I demand, I will feed and keep thy sheep again.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja (Laban) sanoi: mitästä minä sinulle annan? Ja Jakob sanoi: ei sinun pidä mitään antaman minulle. Mutta jos sinä tämän minulle teet, niin minä vielä tästälähin kaitsen ja varjelen sinun laumaas:
verses.king_james: And he said, What shall I give thee? And Jacob said, Thou shalt not give me any thing: if thou wilt do this thing for me, I will again feed and keep thy flock:

verses.latin_vulgate: Gyra omnes greges tuos, et separa cunctas oves varias, et sparso vellere ; quodcumque furvum, et maculosum, variumque fuerit, tam in ovibus quam in capris, erit merces mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶֽעֱבֹ֨ר בְּכׇל־צֹֽאנְךָ֜ הַיּ֗וֹם הָסֵ֨ר מִשָּׁ֜ם כׇּל־שֶׂ֣ה ׀ נָקֹ֣ד וְטָל֗וּא וְכׇל־שֶׂה־חוּם֙ בַּכְּשָׂבִ֔ים וְטָל֥וּא וְנָקֹ֖ד בָּעִזִּ֑ים וְהָיָ֖ה שְׂכָרִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Go round through all thy flocks, and separate all the sheep of divers colours, and speckled; and all that is brown and spotted, and of divers colours, as well among the sheep as among the goats, shall be my wages.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä käyn tänäpänä kaiken sinun laumas lävitse, eroittaen sieltä kaikki pilkulliset ja kirjavat lampaat, ja kaikki hallavat karitsain seassa, ja kirjavat ja pilkulliset vuohten seassa, (mitä sitte kirjavaksi ja pilkulliseksi tulee) se olkoon minun palkkani.
verses.king_james: I will pass through all thy flock to day, removing from thence all the speckled and spotted cattle, and all the brown cattle among the sheep, and the spotted and speckled among the goats: and of such shall be my hire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondebitque mihi cras justitia mea, quando placiti tempus advenerit coram te : et omnia quæ non fuerint varia, et maculosa, et furva, tam in ovibus quam in capris, furti me arguent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעָֽנְתָה־בִּ֤י צִדְקָתִי֙ בְּי֣וֹם מָחָ֔ר כִּֽי־תָב֥וֹא עַל־שְׂכָרִ֖י לְפָנֶ֑יךָ כֹּ֣ל אֲשֶׁר־אֵינֶ֩נּוּ֩ נָקֹ֨ד וְטָל֜וּא בָּֽעִזִּ֗ים וְחוּם֙ בַּכְּשָׂבִ֔ים גָּנ֥וּב ה֖וּא אִתִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And my justice shall answer for me tomorrow before thee, when the time of the bargain shall come; and all that is not of divers colours, and spotted, and brown, as well among the sheep as among the goats, shall accuse me of theft.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minun vanhurskauteni on todistava tästedes minusta, koska se siihen tulee, että minun pitää palkkani sinun nähtes saaman: niin että kaikki, jotka ei ole pilkulliset taikka kirjavat vohlista, eikä hallavat karitsoista, se olkoon varkaus minun tykönäni.
verses.king_james: So shall my righteousness answer for me in time to come, when it shall come for my hire before thy face: every one that is not speckled and spotted among the goats, and brown among the sheep, that shall be counted stolen with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Laban : Gratum habeo quod petis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר לָבָ֖ן הֵ֑ן ל֖וּ יְהִ֥י כִדְבָרֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Laban said: I like well what thou demandest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Laban: katso, joska se olis sanas jälkeen.
verses.king_james: And Laban said, Behold, I would it might be according to thy word.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et separavit in die illa capras, et oves, et hircos, et arietes varios, atque maculosos : cunctum autem gregem unicolorem, id est albi et nigri velleris, tradidit in manu filiorum suorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣סַר בַּיּוֹם֩ הַה֨וּא אֶת־הַתְּיָשִׁ֜ים הָֽעֲקֻדִּ֣ים וְהַטְּלֻאִ֗ים וְאֵ֤ת כׇּל־הָֽעִזִּים֙ הַנְּקֻדּ֣וֹת וְהַטְּלֻאֹ֔ת כֹּ֤ל אֲשֶׁר־לָבָן֙ בּ֔וֹ וְכׇל־ח֖וּם בַּכְּשָׂבִ֑ים וַיִּתֵּ֖ן בְּיַד־בָּנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he separated the same day the she-goats, and the sheep, and the he-goats, and the rams of divers colours, and spotted; and all the flock of one colour, that is, of white and black fleece, he delivered into the hands of his sons.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinä päivänä eroitti hän pilkulliset ja kirjavat kauriit ja kaikki pilkulliset ja kirjavat vuohet, kaikki joissa jotakin valkeutta oli, ja kaikki hallavat karitsat: ja antoi ne lastensa haltuun.
verses.king_james: And he removed that day the he goats that were ringstraked and spotted, and all the she goats that were speckled and spotted, and every one that had some white in it, and all the brown among the sheep, and gave them into the hand of his sons.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et posuit spatium itineris trium dierum inter se et generum, qui pascebat reliquos greges ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֗שֶׂם דֶּ֚רֶךְ שְׁלֹ֣שֶׁת יָמִ֔ים בֵּינ֖וֹ וּבֵ֣ין יַעֲקֹ֑ב וְיַעֲקֹ֗ב רֹעֶ֛ה אֶת־צֹ֥אן לָבָ֖ן הַנּוֹתָרֹֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he set the space of three days journey betwixt himself and his son-in-law, who fed the rest of his flock.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja asetti kolmen päiväkunnan matkan, itsensä ja Jakobin vaiheelle: ja Jakob kaitsi niitä, jotka Labanin laumasta jäivät.
verses.king_james: And he set three days’ journey betwixt himself and Jacob: and Jacob fed the rest of Laban’s flocks.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tollens ergo Jacob virgas populeas virides, et amygdalinas, et ex platanis, ex parte decorticavit eas : detractisque corticibus, in his, quæ spoliata fuerant, candor apparuit : illa vero quæ integra fuerant, viridia permanserunt : atque in hunc modum color effectus est varius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽקַּֽח־ל֣וֹ יַעֲקֹ֗ב מַקַּ֥ל לִבְנֶ֛ה לַ֖ח וְל֣וּז וְעַרְמ֑וֹן וַיְפַצֵּ֤ל בָּהֵן֙ פְּצָל֣וֹת לְבָנ֔וֹת מַחְשֹׂף֙ הַלָּבָ֔ן אֲשֶׁ֖ר עַל־הַמַּקְלֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob took green rods of poplar, and of almond, and of plane-trees, and pilled them in part: so when the bark was taken off, in the parts that were pilled, there appeared whiteness: but the parts that were whole, remained green: and by this means the colour was divers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Jakob otti viherjäisiä haapaisia sauvoja, mandelpuisia ja plataneapuisia; ja kuori niihin valkiat juonteet, valkian paikan paljastamisella, joka sauvain päällä oli.
verses.king_james: And Jacob took him rods of green poplar, and of the hazel and chesnut tree; and pilled white strakes in them, and made the white appear which was in the rods.

verses.latin_vulgate: Posuitque eas in canalibus, ubi effundebatur aqua : ut cum venissent greges ad bibendum, ante oculos haberent virgas, et in aspectu earum conciperent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּצֵּ֗ג אֶת־הַמַּקְלוֹת֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר פִּצֵּ֔ל בָּרְהָטִ֖ים בְּשִֽׁקְת֣וֹת הַמָּ֑יִם אֲשֶׁר֩ תָּבֹ֨אןָ הַצֹּ֤אן לִשְׁתּוֹת֙ לְנֹ֣כַח הַצֹּ֔אן וַיֵּחַ֖מְנָה בְּבֹאָ֥ן לִשְׁתּֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he put them in the troughs, where the water was poured out; that when the flocks should come to drink, they might have the rods before their eyes, and in the sight of them might conceive.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja pani ne sauvat, jotka hän kuorinut oli, laumain eteen, vesiruuhiin, ja juoma-astioihin, lauman eteen, joihin he tulivat juomaan, että he juomalle tultuansa siittäisivät.
verses.king_james: And he set the rods which he had pilled before the flocks in the gutters in the watering troughs when the flocks came to drink, that they should conceive when they came to drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factumque est ut in ipso calore coitus, oves intuerentur virgas, et parerent maculosa, et varia, et diverso colore respersa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֶּחֱמ֥וּ הַצֹּ֖אן אֶל־הַמַּקְל֑וֹת וַתֵּלַ֣דְןָ הַצֹּ֔אן עֲקֻדִּ֥ים נְקֻדִּ֖ים וּטְלֻאִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it came to pass, that in the very heat of coition, the sheep beheld the rods, and brought forth spotted, and of divers colours, and speckled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja niin laumat siittivät niiden sauvain päällä, ja kantoivat pilkullisia, juonteisia ja kirjavia.
verses.king_james: And the flocks conceived before the rods, and brought forth cattle ringstraked, speckled, and spotted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divisitque gregem Jacob, et posuit virgas in canalibus ante oculos arietum : erant autem alba et nigra quæque, Laban ; cetera vero, Jacob, separatis inter se gregibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַכְּשָׂבִים֮ הִפְרִ֣יד יַעֲקֹב֒ וַ֠יִּתֵּ֠ן פְּנֵ֨י הַצֹּ֧אן אֶל־עָקֹ֛ד וְכׇל־ח֖וּם בְּצֹ֣אן לָבָ֑ן וַיָּֽשֶׁת־ל֤וֹ עֲדָרִים֙ לְבַדּ֔וֹ וְלֹ֥א שָׁתָ֖ם עַל־צֹ֥אן לָבָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jacob separated the flock, and put the rods in the troughs before the eyes of the rams; and all the white and the black were Laban's, and the rest were Jacob's, when the flocks were separated one from the other.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Jakob eroitti karitsat, ja asetti lauman kasvot, siinä valkiassa laumassa, niitä pilkullisia ja hallavia päin: ja teki itsellensä eri lauman, ja ei laskenut niitä Labanin lauman sekaan.
verses.king_james: And Jacob did separate the lambs, and set the faces of the flocks toward the ringstraked, and all the brown in the flock of Laban; and he put his own flocks by themselves, and put them not unto Laban’s cattle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur quando primo tempore ascendebantur oves, ponebat Jacob virgas in canalibus aquarum ante oculos arietum et ovium, ut in earum contemplatione conciperent :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֗ה בְּכׇל־יַחֵם֮ הַצֹּ֣אן הַמְקֻשָּׁרוֹת֒ וְשָׂ֨ם יַעֲקֹ֧ב אֶת־הַמַּקְל֛וֹת לְעֵינֵ֥י הַצֹּ֖אן בָּרְהָטִ֑ים לְיַחְמֵ֖נָּה בַּמַּקְלֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: So when the ewes went first to ram, Jacob put the rods in the troughs of water before the eyes of the rams, and of the ewes, that they might conceive while they were looking upon them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja koska se varhain kantava lauma oli sikoillansa, pani Jakob sauvat laumain silmäin eteen ruuhiin: että he siittäisivät sauvain päällä.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, whensoever the stronger cattle did conceive, that Jacob laid the rods before the eyes of the cattle in the gutters, that they might conceive among the rods.

verses.latin_vulgate: quando vero serotina admissura erat, et conceptus extremus, non ponebat eas. Factaque sunt ea quæ erant serotina, Laban : et quæ primi temporis, Jacob.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְהַעֲטִ֥יף הַצֹּ֖אן לֹ֣א יָשִׂ֑ים וְהָיָ֤ה הָעֲטֻפִים֙ לְלָבָ֔ן וְהַקְּשֻׁרִ֖ים לְיַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: But when the later coming was, and the last conceiving, he did not put them. And those that were lateward, became Laban's; and they of the first time, Jacob's.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta koska ne hiljain juoksivat, niin ei hän pannut niitä sisälle. Niin tulivat äpöiset Labanille ja varhain kannetut Jakobille.
verses.king_james: But when the cattle were feeble, he put them not in: so the feebler were Laban’s, and the stronger Jacob’s.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ditatusque est homo ultra modum, et habuit greges multos, ancillas et servos, camelos et asinos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּפְרֹ֥ץ הָאִ֖ישׁ מְאֹ֣ד מְאֹ֑ד וַֽיְהִי־לוֹ֙ צֹ֣אן רַבּ֔וֹת וּשְׁפָחוֹת֙ וַעֲבָדִ֔ים וּגְמַלִּ֖ים וַחֲמֹרִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the man was enriched exceedingly, and he had many flocks, maid-servants and men-servants, camels and asses.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siitä tuli mies sangen äveriääksi, niin että hänellä oli paljo lampaita ja piikoja ja palvelioita, ja kameleja ja aaseja.
verses.king_james: And the man increased exceedingly, and had much cattle, and maidservants, and menservants, and camels, and asses.
